questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
Data set A: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, 8, 11, 11, 14, 14, 14
Data set B: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 11, 11, 14
Data set A and data set B each have 11 values. Which of the following statements best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?
questions.questionText:
Data set A: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, 8, 11, 11, 14, 14, 14
Data set B: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 11, 11, 14
Data set A and data set B each have 11 values. Which of the following statements best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?

choices.text: The median of data set A is greater than the median of data set B.
The median of data set A is less than the median of data set B.
The medians of data sets A and B are equal.
There is not enough information to compare the medians.

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$f(x)=-9x^2 + 113x$
The function $f$ models the depth below sea level, in meters, of a certain seal $x$ minutes after the seal dives into the water. What is the best interpretation of $f(2)= 190$ in this context?
questions.questionText:
$f(x)=-9x^2 + 113x$
The function $f$ models the depth below sea level, in meters, of a certain seal $x$ minutes after the seal dives into the water. What is the best interpretation of $f(2)= 190$ in this context?

choices.text: 2 minutes after the seal dives into the water, its estimated depth is 2 meters below sea level.
The seal reaches an estimated maximum depth of 190 meters below sea level.
2 minutes after the seal dives into the water, its estimated depth is 190 meters below sea level.
Approximately 2 minutes after diving into the water, the seal returns to sea level.

questions.prompt: To cut a lawn, Antwan charges a fee of \$10.00 for his equipment and \$8.50 per hour spent cutting a lawn. Taylor charges a fee of \$7.00 for her equipment and \$9.50 per hour spent cutting a lawn. If $x$ represents the number of hours spent cutting a lawn, what are all the values of $x$ for which Taylor's total charge is greater than Antwan's total charge?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In a certain state, the population of pheasants, a type of bird, is estimated each year by counting the number of pheasants observed along certain roads in the state. On average, each year from 2005 to 2015 the number of pheasants counted per mile of road decreased by 3.5% of the number of pheasants per mile of road the previous year. Based on this average, if there were 6.22 pheasants per mile of road in this state in 2005, which of the following best approximates the number of pheasants per mile of road in 2015?
questions.questionText: In a certain state, the population of pheasants, a type of bird, is estimated each year by counting the number of pheasants observed along certain roads in the state. On average, each year from 2005 to 2015 the number of pheasants counted per mile of road decreased by 3.5% of the number of pheasants per mile of road the previous year. Based on this average, if there were 6.22 pheasants per mile of road in this state in 2005, which of the following best approximates the number of pheasants per mile of road in 2015?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
Data set $F$ consists of 55 integers, where the value of each integer is between 150 and 260. Data set $G$ consists of the same 55 integers in data set $F$ as well as the integer 10. Which of the following must be less for data set $G$ than for data set $F$?
I. The mean
II. The median

I. The mean
II. The median

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A gallery had $x$ artifacts in a collection. When more artifacts were added to the collection, the number of artifacts increased by 149%. What is the resulting number of artifacts in the collection in terms of $x$?
questions.questionText: A gallery had $x$ artifacts in a collection. When more artifacts were added to the collection, the number of artifacts increased by 149%. What is the resulting number of artifacts in the collection in terms of $x$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = a^x + b$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants and $a > 0$. In the $xy$-plane, the graph of $y = f(x)$ has a $y$-intercept at $(0, -20)$ and passes through the point $(2, 43)$. What is the value of $a - b$?
questions.questionText: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = a^x + b$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants and $a > 0$. In the $xy$-plane, the graph of $y = f(x)$ has a $y$-intercept at $(0, -20)$ and passes through the point $(2, 43)$. What is the value of $a - b$?

questions.prompt: One gallon of sealant costs \$23 and will cover $250$ square feet of a surface. A deck has a total surface area of $d$ square feet. Which equation represents the cost $c$, in dollars, of the sealant needed to cover the deck twice?

The table gives the perimeters of similar triangles $TUV$ and $XYZ$, where $\overline{TU}$ corresponds to $\overline{XY}$. The length of $\overline{TU}$ is 16.
What is the length of $\overline{XY}$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A piece of string with a length of $96$ inches is cut into two parts. One part has a length of $x$ inches, and the other part has a length of $y$ inches. The value of $x$ is 6 more than 4 times the value of $y$. What is the value of $x$?
questions.questionText: A piece of string with a length of $96$ inches is cut into two parts. One part has a length of $x$ inches, and the other part has a length of $y$ inches. The value of $x$ is 6 more than 4 times the value of $y$. What is the value of $x$?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The area of a triangle is $270$ square centimeters. The length of the base of the triangle is $12$ centimeters greater than the height of the triangle. What is the height, in centimeters, of the triangle?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: An employee earns \$30 per hour for the first 40 hours worked in a week. For each hour worked over 40 hours, the employee earns \$45 per hour. What is the least number of whole hours that the employee must work in a week to earn at least \$1,610?

questions.prompt:
$17(x-16)=-16(x + 17)$
How many distinct real solutions does the given equation have?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt:
$22x-21y=43$
$5x+14y=59$
The solution to the given system of equations is $(x, y)=\left(\frac{p}{59}, \frac{w}{826}\right)$, where $p$ and $w$ are integers. What is the value of $p$?

questions.prompt: The table shows three values of $ x $ and their corresponding values of $ y $. There is a linear relationship between $ x $ and $ y $. What is the $ x $-coordinate of the $ x $-intercept of the graph that represents this linear relationship in the $ xy $-plane?

questions.testName: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: In the figure, $\overline{LQ}$ intersects $\overline{MP}$ at point R, and $\overline{LM}$ is parallel to $\overline{PQ}$. The lengths of $\overline{MR}$, $\overline{LR}$, and $\overline{RP}$ are 9, 10, and 19, respectively. What is the length of $\overline{LQ}$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Cristina was elected to the city council. Each city council person is required to attend at least 21 of the 27 scheduled council meetings in a year to keep their position. Which inequality represents the number of city council meetings, $x$, Cristina must attend in a year to keep her position on the city council?

questions.prompt: The function $f(x) = -40x + 280$ gives the predicted height above the ground $f(x)$, in feet, of a model airplane $x$ minutes after it begins to descend. What is the predicted height above the ground, in feet, of the model airplane 4 minutes after it begins to descend?
questions.questionText: The function $f(x) = -40x + 280$ gives the predicted height above the ground $f(x)$, in feet, of a model airplane $x$ minutes after it begins to descend. What is the predicted height above the ground, in feet, of the model airplane 4 minutes after it begins to descend?

questions.prompt: The given equation relates the distinct positive numbers $u$, $w$, and $x$: $u = -\frac{w}{188x}$. Which equation correctly expresses $w$ in terms of $u$ and $x$?
questions.questionText: The given equation relates the distinct positive numbers $u$, $w$, and $x$: $u = -\frac{w}{188x}$. Which equation correctly expresses $w$ in terms of $u$ and $x$?

questions.prompt: In triangle $ABC$, angle $B$ is a right angle. The length of side $AB$ is $10\sqrt{39}$ and the length of side $BC$ is $24\sqrt{39}$. What is the length of side $AC$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: To cut a lawn, Antwan charges a fee of \$10.00 for his equipment and \$8.50 per hour spent cutting a lawn. Taylor charges a fee of \$7.00 for her equipment and \$9.50 per hour spent cutting a lawn. If $x$ represents the number of hours spent cutting a lawn, what are all the values of $x$ for which Taylor's total charge is greater than Antwan's total charge?

questions.questionText: In the $xy$-plane, line $p$ has a slope of $\frac{3}{4}$ and an $x$-intercept of $(16, 0)$. What is the $y$-coordinate of the $y$-intercept of line $p$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The given table shows the number of even and odd integers contained in list A and list B. No integer is contained in both lists. What fraction of the even integers represented in the table are in list A?
questions.questionText: The given table shows the number of even and odd integers contained in list A and list B. No integer is contained in both lists. What fraction of the even integers represented in the table are in list A?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In a certain state, the population of pheasants, a type of bird, is estimated each year by counting the number of pheasants observed along certain roads in the state. On average, each year from 2005 to 2015 the number of pheasants counted per mile of road decreased by 3.5% of the number of pheasants per mile of road the previous year. Based on this average, if there were 6.22 pheasants per mile of road in this state in 2005, which of the following best approximates the number of pheasants per mile of road in 2015?
questions.questionText: In a certain state, the population of pheasants, a type of bird, is estimated each year by counting the number of pheasants observed along certain roads in the state. On average, each year from 2005 to 2015 the number of pheasants counted per mile of road decreased by 3.5% of the number of pheasants per mile of road the previous year. Based on this average, if there were 6.22 pheasants per mile of road in this state in 2005, which of the following best approximates the number of pheasants per mile of road in 2015?

questions.prompt:
$x^2 - 12x - 13 = 0$
One solution to the given equation can be written as $x = h - \frac{1}{2}\sqrt{k}$, where $h$ and $k$ are integer constants. What is the value of $h + k$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
Data set $F$ consists of 55 integers, where the value of each integer is between 150 and 260. Data set $G$ consists of the same 55 integers in data set $F$ as well as the integer 10. Which of the following must be less for data set $G$ than for data set $F$?
I. The mean
II. The median

I. The mean
II. The median

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A gallery had $x$ artifacts in a collection. When more artifacts were added to the collection, the number of artifacts increased by 149%. What is the resulting number of artifacts in the collection in terms of $x$?
questions.questionText: A gallery had $x$ artifacts in a collection. When more artifacts were added to the collection, the number of artifacts increased by 149%. What is the resulting number of artifacts in the collection in terms of $x$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = a^x + b$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants and $a > 0$. In the $xy$-plane, the graph of $y = f(x)$ has a $y$-intercept at $(0, -20)$ and passes through the point $(2, 43)$. What is the value of $a - b$?
questions.questionText: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = a^x + b$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants and $a > 0$. In the $xy$-plane, the graph of $y = f(x)$ has a $y$-intercept at $(0, -20)$ and passes through the point $(2, 43)$. What is the value of $a - b$?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: One gallon of sealant costs \$23 and will cover $250$ square feet of a surface. A deck has a total surface area of $d$ square feet. Which equation represents the cost $c$, in dollars, of the sealant needed to cover the deck twice?
questions.questionText: One gallon of sealant costs \$23 and will cover $250$ square feet of a surface. A deck has a total surface area of $d$ square feet. Which equation represents the cost $c$, in dollars, of the sealant needed to cover the deck twice?

questions.testName: November 2024 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A right square pyramid has a height of $11$ centimeters (cm). A second right square pyramid has a height of $22$ centimeters. The area of the bases of each of the two pyramids is $100$ $cm^2$. Which of the following is closest to the difference in the surface area of the second pyramid and the surface area of the first pyramid, in $cm^2$?

questions.prompt: An object's speed is increasing at a rate of $5.70$ meters per second squared. What is this rate, in <u> miles per minute squared, </u> rounded to the nearest tenth? (Use 1 mile = 1,609 meters.)

questions.prompt: For triangle $ABC$, the length of $\overline{AB}$ is 11 less than the length of $\overline{AC}$. Point D (not shown) lies on $\overline{AC}$ such that $\overline{BD}$ (not shown) is perpendicular to $\overline{AC}$. What is the value of $\frac{BC}{BD}$?

questions.prompt:
$\frac{1}{c x} = \frac{x}{148} + \frac{1}{c}$
In the given equation, $c$ is a constant. The equation has exactly one solution. What is the value of $c$?
